subject: Cumulative and Survival Distribution Functions
question: Determine whether the given values can serve as the values of a distribution function of a random variable with the range $x = 1, 2, 3, 4$. $F(1) = 0.3$, $F(2) = 0.5$, $F(3) = 0.8$, and $F(4) = 1.2$
explanation: No because $F(4)$ exceeds 1. All probability questions can be answered in terms of the c.d.f. Proposition 25.5 For any random variable $X$ and any real number $a$, we have $\Pr(X > a) = 1-F(a)$. Proof. Let $A = \{x\in S : X(s)\le a\}$. Then $A^c = \{s\in S : X(s) > a\}$. We have $\Pr(X > a) = \Pr(A^c) = 1-\Pr(A) = 1-\Pr(X\le a) = 1-F(a)$
